author: @Modification102
text:
The one thinh AI is totally incapable of replicating, which stands out if you are listening for it, is vocal tone. It can match speech speed, but it has a very hard time replicating natural peaks and lows in a vocal range.

So if you want to counter it, play in its weak spot. Ensure you emphasise vocal range with clear highs and lows. That way, any AI will come out monotone.

replies.author: @JJW3
replies.text: If you watch any of the military history or weaponry content channels you quickly spot that AI has a hard time with acronyms and especially caliber designations. AI fails to pronounce them the way a natural human or an expert on the topic would.

author: @karenhawkins8061
text: Another channel had the same problem, The Lore Lodge's Aiden had his likeness and voice cloned (by india) and was told that he does not own his own likeness including his voice! - Also found this  technically, anyone on YouTube can clone another creator's voice using artificial intelligence (AI) tools, but doing so without permission is legally risky and often violates YouTube’s policies. While platforms like ElevenLabs allow users to create a clone from just minutes of audio, doing this to impersonate another person can lead to legal action, content take-downs, and demonetization.

author: @crystalvision1111
text: They are doing to tons of small channels as well. They actually steal entire long form videos audio, alter the voice slightly, and reupppload it as their own! Youtube wont do anything, and since you are out of the country, it is so hard to find a lawyer (plus expensive) even WILLING to take your case. So, nothing happens. Even if you have your channel name trademarked, youtube wont do a thing when they copy that as well. This needs to change. Its not right, and it is not fair.

author: @djangodjango-k9k
text:
I know Neal Mohan was born in the US
But I find it rather funny that ever since he became CEO the influx of ever growing Indian channels, Indian focused content, and other stuff that is just outright theft of other channels purposed over to Indian viewers/demographics has grown especially with the use of AI 
It makes me wonder sometimes if the theft of stuff that generates so many views which then generates ad revenue which then of course lines YouTubes pockets isn't to some degree condoned and purposefully ignored to capitalize as much as possible on the ad revenue as well as a possible strange prop to the Indian economy of the area the fraudsters live at before enough of a stink is raised to get channels delisted and removed
Which then of course gets replaced right away by a copy cat channel doing the same stuff

author: @joonsmelody
text: I fell for one of these videos one day late last week and subbed automatically, simply because I thought it was something new you all were doing together!  But I wouldn't be lying when i tell you I still got bored halfway thru and decided to unsub.  Because even tho I was only half-paying attn to it while getting ready to go out, I still managed to feel like the content was just droning on & on with no heart or direction.  I knew subconsciously that something wasn't right.  I will take note of that feeling for next time.
